hero.headline: Happiness is Submission to God
hero.description: You are invited to join a unified religion for all people—Submission to God alone. All world religions have been corrupted by human innovations and idolatry, but God's plan is now being fulfilled. A messenger has arrived to purify the scriptures and unite them into one universal message. Whether you are Christian, Jewish, Muslim, Hindu, Buddhist, or of any faith, you can become a Submitter by devoting your worship to God alone and living righteously. This is the era where God's messages have been consolidated into one religion for all humanity. Here you'll discover what Submission means and how it leads to happiness in this life and redemption in the Hereafter.

services.finalTestamentDesc: Complete translation with word-by-word analysis, appendices, and search

miracle.title: Miracle
miracle.subtitle: Code 19
miracle.cardDescription: The Quran is mathematically composed around the prime number 19. The opening statement — Bismillah Al-Rahman Al-Raheem — consists of exactly 19 Arabic letters. The Quran contains 114 chapters (19 × 6). The first revelation (96:1–5) has 19 words. The word "God" appears 2,698 times (19 × 142). This interlocking numerical code, discovered by Dr. Rashad Khalifa in 1974, provides a built-in authentication that no human could have designed 14 centuries ago — a divine signature confirming the Quran's authorship and protecting it from tampering.
miracle.youtubeDesc: A visual introduction to the mathematical miracle of the Quran.

home.aboutText: WikiSubmission is a faith-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization...

downloads.heading: Downloads
downloads.description: Free books, research papers, and resources for Submission.

miracle.metadataDescription: Discover Code 19 — the mathematical miracle of the Quran, a built-in divine authentication discovered by Dr. Rashad Khalifa in 1974.
miracle.heading: The Mathematical Miracle of the Quran
miracle.description: A built-in divine authentication — an interlocking numerical code woven throughout the Quran, centered on the prime number 19.
miracle.intro1Before: In 1974, Dr. Rashad Khalifa — a biochemist and Muslim scholar — used a computer to analyze the Arabic text of the Quran and discovered an extraordinary mathematical structure built around the number 19, mentioned in
miracle.intro2: The patterns are not superficial — they are deeply interlocked across the entire text. The number of chapters, the letter counts of the opening statement, the word frequencies of key divine names, and the structure of the earliest revelations all converge on precise multiples of 19. The statistical probability of these patterns occurring by chance is vanishingly small.
miracle.intro3: This mathematical code serves as a divine signature: it proves the Quran was authored by God, confirms the integrity of the text that has been preserved to this day, and provides a rational, verifiable proof of the scripture's authenticity that anyone can examine.
miracle.youtubeTitle: Code 19: The Mathematical Miracle of the Quran
miracle.factBismillahDetail: The opening statement of the Quran — Bismillah Al-Rahman Al-Raheem — consists of exactly 19 Arabic letters.
miracle.factChaptersDetail: The Quran contains 114 chapters, an exact multiple of 19.
miracle.factRevelationDetail: The first five verses ever revealed (96:1–5) contain exactly 19 words.
miracle.factGodDetail: The word "God" (Allah) appears exactly 2,698 times throughout the Quran — a precise multiple of 19.
miracle.factChapter96Detail: Chapter 96 (Al-Alaq), the first chapter to be revealed, contains exactly 19 verses.
miracle.factDiscoveryDetail: Dr. Rashad Khalifa used a computer to analyze the Quran and discovered the mathematical code based on the number 19.
miracle.appendixDesc: Appendix 1 of the Final Testament provides a detailed, comprehensive treatment of the mathematical miracle with hundreds of examples and proofs.
miracle.visualBismillahTranslation: In the name of God, Most Gracious, Most Merciful.

blog.heading: Blog
blog.description: Articles, reflections, and research from the WikiSubmission community.
blog.error: Blog posts are temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.
blog.noResultsHelp: Try a different search or category.
blog.tutorialStep1Tip: Make sure to check your spam folder if you don't see the invitation within 24 hours.
blog.tutorialStep1Success: Your request is being processed by our administration team.
blog.tutorialStep2Tip: Using a Google or GitHub account linked to your email is the fastest way to log in.
blog.tutorialStep2Success: Secure connection established with Sanity Studio.
blog.tutorialStep3Tip: A complete profile helps establish your authority as a contributor.
blog.tutorialStep3Success: Your author identity is now configured.
blog.tutorialStep4Tip: This manual step ensures all published research meets our quality standards.
blog.tutorialStep4Success: Writing permissions unlocked. Welcome to the team!
blog.tutorialStep5Tip: You can switch languages at any time to see how your article appears in different locales.
blog.tutorialStep6Tip: Published articles can still be edited; simply make your changes and click Update.
blog.tutorialStep6Success: Your research is now part of the global archive!
blog.tutorialStep1Content1: All contributor accounts must be manually approved by our team.
blog.tutorialStep1Content2: Please contact us at <link>contact@wikisubmission.org</link> with your preferred email address.
blog.tutorialStep1Content3: Once approved, you will receive an invitation email leading you to the authentication page:
blog.tutorialStep2Content1: Sign in using the <bold>exact same email address</bold> you provided for approval.
blog.tutorialStep2Content2: You will be asked to accept Sanity's Terms of Service. Sanity is the secure infrastructure we use to host and manage our research archives.
blog.tutorialStep2Content3: Afterwards, you will be redirected to the WikiSubmission Studio.
blog.tutorialStep3Content1: Inside the Studio, click on the <bold>My Profile</bold> tab in the sidebar.
blog.tutorialStep3Content2: Please fill in your <bold>First Name</bold> and <bold>Last Name</bold>. We also recommend adding a short biography to introduce yourself to your readers.
blog.tutorialStep4Content1: After setting up your profile, our moderators will verify your credentials.
blog.tutorialStep4Content2: Once verified, the <bold>Articles</bold> section will unlock, allowing you to start drafting your work:
blog.tutorialStep5Content1: Click <bold>+ New article</bold> to begin. Essential: Use the <bold>Translations</bold> menu to set the language of your article.
blog.tutorialStep5Content2: Setting the correct language ensures your research is indexed correctly for global readers.
blog.tutorialStep6Content1: Once your article is ready, click the <bold>Publish</bold> button in the bottom right corner.
blog.tutorialStep6Content2: Congratulations! Your contribution is now live and accessible to the global community on WikiSubmission.

practices.metadataDescription: Prayer times and Ramadan schedule for any location.
practices.heading: Life as a submitter
practices.description: Prayer times, Ramadan schedule, and Zakat calculator — tools to support your daily worship.
practices.zakatDescription: A mandatory charity of 2.5% on all liquid assets. The Quranic mandate (6:141) emphasizes immediate distribution at the point of receipt, ensuring constant flow of capital to those in need.
practices.prayerTimesDesc: Establish the five daily contact prayers, precisely timed to the sun's position.
practices.zakatQuote: “They ask you about giving: say, 'The charity you give shall go to the parents, the relatives, the orphans, the poor, and the traveling alien.' Any good you do, GOD is fully aware thereof.”
practices.card1Desc: The meaning, times, and postures of the five daily prayers.
practices.card2Desc: Who owes Zakat, how to calculate it, and who to give it to.
practices.card3Desc: The purpose of fasting and how to observe Ramadan correctly.
practices.card4Desc: The pilgrimage to Mecca — its rites and spiritual significance.

zakatCalculator.title: Zakat Asset Analyzer
zakatCalculator.description: Based on the Quran: 2.5% of income is owed the moment you receive it — no waiting period, no minimum threshold.
zakatCalculator.recommendationBody: This amount is below the typical distribution threshold. We recommend aggregating small Zakat dues over several receipts to optimize impact, or fulfilling the obligation preemptively.
zakatCalculator.protocolBody: When you receive income, 2.5% belongs to those in need—give it immediately or as soon as possible to maintain system integrity.
zakatCalculator.variableBody: No minimum threshold (Nisab) or waiting period (Hawl) is required according to the Quranic directive (6:141).

footer.missionDesc: Faith-based nonprofit organization providing free and open-source technology, educational resources, and creative works in the cause of God.
footer.supportBody: We operate as a registered 501(c)(3) public charity (EIN 39-4876245). Every contribution funds the work directly and is fully tax-deductible.
footer.verse112: Proclaim, “He is the One and only GOD. The Absolute GOD. Never did He beget. Nor was He begotten. None equals Him.”
footer.madeForCause: Made for the cause of God
footer.poweredBody: We are committed to developing free and open-source tools & technology. No ads, no paywalls. To help us in this cause, you can <donateLink>make a contribution</donateLink>. As a 501(c)(3) registered nonprofit (EIN: 39-4876245), your donations may be tax-deductible depending on your country.
footer.connectBody: Our team is growing. If you would like to get involved, or have any questions, connect with us on <discordLink>Discord</discordLink> or email us at <emailLink>{email}</emailLink>.
footer.connectShortBody: Our team is growing and is working 24/7 to further research, technology, and art in the cause of God.

verseOfDay.quran1Text: Surely, those who believe, those who are Jewish, the Christians, and the converts; anyone who believes in GOD, believes in the Last Day, and leads a righteous life, will receive their recompense from their Lord.
verseOfDay.quran2Text: The only religion approved by GOD is Submission.
verseOfDay.quran3Text: Most blessed is the One who revealed the Statute Book to His servant, so he can serve as a warner to the whole world.
verseOfDay.ot1Text: Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is one.
verseOfDay.ot1Footnote: Cited by Jesus as the first commandment.
verseOfDay.ot2Text: I am the LORD: that is my name: and my glory will I not give to another.
verseOfDay.ot2Footnote: One of the clearest monotheistic declarations in the Tanakh.
verseOfDay.ot3Text: To do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God.
verseOfDay.nt1Text: The first of all the commandments is, Hear, O Israel; The Lord our God is one Lord.
verseOfDay.nt2Text: And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God.
verseOfDay.nt2Footnote: Spoken by Jesus in prayer to the Father.
verseOfDay.nt3Text: Thou believest that there is one God; thou doest well.

practices.prayerDesc: Five daily contacts with God — observed at their proper astronomical times. The second pillar of Submission, alongside belief itself.
practices.zakatQuote: “They ask you about giving: say, 'The charity you give shall go to the parents, the relatives, the orphans, the poor, and the traveling alien.' Any good you do, GOD is fully aware thereof.”
practices.zakatDesc: A 2.5% charity on every income, paid the moment you receive it — to the categories God has specified. No threshold, no lunar year.
practices.ramadanDesc: The month of Ramadan is the month in which the Quran was revealed, providing guidance for the people and clear teachings.
practices.fastingVerse183: O you who believe, fasting is decreed for you, as it was decreed for those before you, that you may attain salvation.
practices.fastingVerse184: Specific days (are designated for fasting); if one is ill or traveling, an equal number of other days shall be substituted.
practices.fastingVerse185: Ramadan is the month in which the Quran was revealed, providing guidance for the people, and clear teachings, and the statute book.
practices.fastingVerse186: When My servants ask you about Me, I am always near. I respond to their prayers when they pray to Me.
practices.fastingVerse187: Permitted for you during the nights of fasting is sexual intercourse with your wives... You may eat and drink until the white thread of light becomes distinguishable from the dark thread of night at dawn.
practices.hajjDesc: Commemorating Abraham's exemplary submission to God, Hajj is observed during the four Sacred Months — Zul-Hijjah, Muharram, Safar, and Rabi' I.
practices.hajjQuote: Proclaim that the people shall observe Hajj pilgrimage. They will come to you walking or riding on various exhausted mounts, coming from the farthest locations.

games.lobbyLede: Practice and memorize through procedurally generated rounds. Every round has a deterministic variant ID, so leaderboards stay fair and rounds are shareable.
games.fillBlankSub: Read a passage with words removed. Pick or type the missing ones.
games.fillBlankIntro: Pick a passage, choose your difficulty and round size, then fill in the blanks. Your score depends on accuracy, difficulty, and hints used.
games.comingSoonPickers: Passage / difficulty / size picker coming soon.
games.noPassages: No passages are available yet. Check back soon.
games.loadError: Could not load passages. Please try again.
games.startError: Could not start the round. Please try again.
games.difficultyHardHint: Type the word, forgiving of inflection
games.difficultyAdaptiveHint: Difficulty chosen for you based on your history
games.sizeAdaptiveHint: Round length chosen for you based on your history
games.submitError: Could not submit your round. Please try again.
games.rateLimited: You are submitting too fast. Please wait a moment and try again.
games.shareText: I scored {score} on Fill the Blank ({correct}/{total} correct).
games.shareFailed: Could not copy the share link.
games.resultExpired: This result is no longer in memory. Play another round or view your history.
games.emptyBoard: No scores yet. Be the first.

meActivity.title: Activity
meActivity.lede: Recent searches and verses you have browsed. Stored only with your consent and removed when you revoke it or clear this page.

adminGames.notAuthorizedDesc: This page is restricted to the games editorial team. Ask an administrator to grant you the games_editor permission from the admin access page.
adminGames.fillBlankTileBody: Curate passages and review GROQ proposals. Approve, reject, or flag for refinement.
adminGames.maintenanceTileBody: Word frequency tables and lemma data for all languages. Rebuild after adding a language or correcting translation text.
adminGames.editorialDesc: Approve, reject, or flag GROQ-proposed passages for the Fill-the-Blank catalog. Approved passages become playable immediately.
adminGames.maintenanceDesc: Word frequencies and lemma data for the fill-blank game. Neither affects curation or passage review.
adminGames.freqSectionNote: Counts how often each word appears in a translation. Used to score blank difficulty (common words = Easy, rare = Hard). Rebuild after adding a new language or correcting translation text.
adminGames.loadError: Could not load passages. Use the filters to retry.

ablution.verse56Full: O you who believe, when you observe the Contact Prayers (Salat), you shall: (1) wash your faces, (2) wash your arms to the elbows, (3) wipe your heads, and (4) wash your feet to the ankles. If you were unclean due to sexual orgasm, you shall bathe. If you are ill, or traveling, or had any digestive excretion (urinary, fecal, or gas), or had (sexual) contact with the women, and you cannot find water, you shall observe the dry ablution (Tayammum) by touching clean dry soil, then rubbing your faces and hands. GOD does not wish to make the religion difficult for you; He wishes to cleanse you and to perfect His blessing upon you, that you may be appreciative.
ablution.step1Desc: Before beginning, silently state your intention to perform ablution in preparation for the Contact Prayer.
ablution.step1Detail: Pure intention (Niyyah) is the spiritual foundation of all worship in Submission.
ablution.step2Desc: The first physical step is to wash your face completely.
ablution.step2Detail: Ensure water reaches all parts of the face, from hairline to chin and ear to ear.
ablution.step3Desc: Wash your right arm up to and including the elbow, then repeat with the left arm.
ablution.step3Detail: The Quranic command specifies "your arms to the elbows" [5:6].
ablution.step4Desc: Wipe your head with wet hands.
ablution.step4Detail: The head is to be "wiped" (Masah) with wet hands, not washed under running water.
ablution.step5Desc: Wash your right foot up to the ankle, then repeat with the left foot.
ablution.step5Detail: This final step completes the physical purification required for the Contact Prayer.
ablution.footnote1Text: If you were unclean due to sexual orgasm, you shall bathe.
ablution.footnote2Text: If you are ill, or traveling, or had any digestive excretion (urinary, fecal, or gas), or had (sexual) contact with the women, and you cannot find water, you shall observe the dry ablution (Tayammum) by touching clean dry soil, then rubbing your faces and hands.
ablution.footnote3Text: GOD does not wish to make the religion difficult for you; He wishes to cleanse you and to perfect His blessing upon you, that you may be appreciative.

meSettings.title: Settings
meSettings.consentBody: We record the chapters you browse, the verse ranges you open, and the text you search for. This data powers your activity page and nothing else. It is never shared or used to identify you across services.
meSettings.clearBody: Delete every activity event we have recorded for your account.

notifications.heading: Notifications
notifications.body: Get reminders and updates on this device. You can turn them off at any time.
notifications.unsupported: This browser does not support notifications.
notifications.blocked: Notifications are blocked in your browser settings.

export.heading: Data export
export.queued: Your export is being prepared. We will email {email} with a Google Drive link within ~15 minutes.
export.sent: Last export sent {when}. You can request another in {eta}.
export.disclosure: The export file is hosted on Google Drive. The link is delivered by email to {email}, shared only with you, and limited to one request per 24h.
